factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Relief (Iran Deal Hopes)
factors.summary: Trump delayed planned Iran strike at Gulf allies' request, citing 'good chance' of nuclear deal. Oil prices fell on optimism, reducing stagflation risk for industrial cyclicals (CAT, BA, HON).

factors.name: Japan GDP Beat + Inflation Run Hot
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP annualized +2.1% (vs 1.7% exp), QoQ +0.5% (vs 0.4% exp), but price index at 3.4% y/y aligns with prior. Signals global growth resilience but confirms sticky inflation—positive for Dow industrials but negative for rate-sensitive dividend payers.

factors.name: RBA Inflation Hawkishness
factors.summary: RBA's Hunter warned elevated risk of inflation expectations drifting higher via oil pass-through; domestic cost pressures stretched. Signals central banks tightening bias persists despite geopolitical relief, bearish for equities and USD-positive.

factors.name: Bond Rout Continuation & Rising Yields
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley warns bond volatility could trigger first meaningful equity correction since late March. Gilts (UK) sold off sharply; USD posted best week since Iran conflict (MUFG: +1.4%). Rising long-end yields compress Dow financials (GS, JPM) and dividend yields.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations (Warsh Hawkish Shift)
factors.summary: MUFG notes hawkish Fed chair Kevin Warsh signals shifted market rate expectations upward; hotter inflation data + higher yields combined. Headwind for multiple expansion in rate-sensitive Dow names (utilities, REITs).

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: Dollar posted best week in ~3 months (+1.4% per MUFG). Bullish for multinational Dow names with ~50% international revenue, but headwind for foreign earnings translation if sustained.

factors.name: Dow Mixed Close (Monday)
factors.summary: Dow closed higher on Iran relief headline, but S&P 500 and Nasdaq lower. Index-specific weakness in tech/growth drags broader sentiment; industrial rotation into cyclicals not yet sustained.

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility
factors.summary: Oil fell on Iran deal hopes but remains elevated. Strait of Hormuz closure risks persist; Goldman cuts recession odds to 25% (from 30%) citing contained impact. Mixed input-cost signal—relief near-term, tail risk remains.

factors.name: Goldman Sachs 12m Recession Probability Cut
factors.summary: Goldman cuts US recession odds to 25% from 30%, citing resilient activity and easing financial conditions despite Hormuz closure. Supports Dow cyclical outlook if sustained.

factors.name: Forward Guidance Void
factors.summary: No recent Dow heavyweight earnings or guidance reported in this window (Intel CEO mentions foundry momentum; ServiceNow beat). CAT, BA, GS, JPM forward outlook not refreshed—limits conviction on industrial/financial strength.

factors.name: Musk/OpenAI Verdict + Tech Sentiment
factors.summary: Musk loses OpenAI case after jury finds he waited too long; vows appeal. Minimal direct Dow impact (no MSFT exposure in Dow, Apple/Nvidia not in index), but highlights AI narrative volatility and legal risk for high-multiple names.

factors.name: China Trade Resilience but Weak Domestic Demand
factors.summary: DBS notes strong exports but subdued consumption, investment, credit. Headwind for Dow export cyclicals (CAT, BA, MMM) if China softness persists; tail risk to global demand.

factors.name: US-Canada Defense Suspension
factors.summary: Trump suspended NORAD agreement dating to WWII, signaling isolationist policy tilt. Long-term headwind for defense contractors (BA, LMT not in Dow but sets tone) and trade relationships; near-term noise.

factors.name: Upcoming RBA Minutes & Hunter Speech
factors.summary: RBA minutes (today Asia time) and Hunter speech (0025 GMT) will clarify tightening bias. If hawkish confirmed, bearish for risk assets; dovish reprieve bullish. Event risk within 24h.

factors.name: Price Action: Minimal Intraday Move
factors.summary: US30 +0.15% (4h), -0.01% (24h). Micro move despite massive headline volatility (Iran, rates, Japan GDP, RBA, oil). Suggests equilibrium confusion; chop likely to persist into RBA/Warsh clarification.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between two offsetting macro crosscurrents: Iran deal optimism and oil relief (bullish for cyclicals) versus accelerating bond selloff, sticky inflation, and hawkish Fed pivot (bearish for yields/multiples). Japan's better-than-expected Q1 GDP supports global growth, but RBA's inflation warnings and rising long-end yields signal central bank tightening persists despite geopolitical de-escalation. USD strength (+1.4% last week) favors multinational Dow names but also signals real yields are grinding higher, compressing valuations. Monday's mixed close (Dow green, S&P/Nadaq red) and micro 24h price action (-0.01%) reflect investor indecision—headline vol without conviction. The decisive catalysts are RBA minutes/Hunter speech (next 24h) and Warsh Fed confirmation; a hawkish hold will likely reignite bond weakness and cap equity upside.
catalysts_ahead: RBA Minutes release (next ~24h, Sydney timezone)
RBA's Sarah Hunter speech at Bloomberg Forum, Sydney (0025 GMT/2025 US ET, next ~24h)
US CPI or PCE inflation data (if due this week; check calendar)
Fed Chair Kevin Warsh public remarks or testimony (market pricing in hawkish shift)
Dow heavyweight earnings (JPM, GS, UNH, MSFT, GE, CAT, BA pending — check for earnings dates)
China PMI or trade data refresh
US ISM Manufacturing PMI (monthly, if due next week)
Trump-Iran negotiation updates or military action reversal
screening.risk_warning: If RBA or Warsh confirm dovish pivot (rates pause/cut), long-end yields collapse and Dow cyclicals rally hard (false signal). Conversely, if Iran talks fail and geopolitical risk resurges, oil spikes, stagflation fears return, and Dow defensives (dividend payers) underperform growth. China data deterioration would undercut cyclicals (CAT, BA) and invalidate Goldman's recession-odds reprieve. Extended bond selloff could trigger forced equity liquidation, as documented by Morgan Stanley—watch credit spreads for stress signals.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (headline-driven chop, no sustained directional bias). Wait for RBA/Warsh clarity before entering size; Dow likely to consolidate 49,400–49,900 range. On dovish surprise, long Dow cyclicals on retest of 49,500. On hawkish hold, short on retest of 49,800 resistance. Avoid chasing here; risk-reward is poor until macro fog clears within 24–48h.

factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical De-escalation
factors.summary: Trump postponed planned Iran strike at request of Gulf allies (Bloomberg, 04:49 UTC), reducing immediate tail-risk premium. Oil fell 2% on news (FXStreet, 04:18 UTC), easing input-cost pressures on industrials and transportation.

factors.name: Bond Yield / Real Rate Repricing
factors.summary: US 10Y yields flirting with 2007 highs (Bloomberg, 03:49 UTC); bond market rout ongoing ('Great Bond Car Wreck in Slow Motion'). Higher real yields typically compress equity valuations, especially dividend-heavy Dow names.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Bias Intensifying
factors.summary: Bond market is pricing elevated odds of Fed rate hikes (MarketWatch, 02:02 UTC); Reuters estimate shows inflation expectations drifting higher. Hawkish Fed expectations support DXY (FXStreet, 01:59 UTC) but pressure equity multiples.

factors.name: Global Growth Mixed / Energy Shock Risk
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP beat at 2.1% annualized (Investinglive, 01:39 UTC), but analysts warn Iran war energy shock threatens momentum and BOJ rate-hike delay. RBA eight of nine members backed May hike citing inflation risks from Gulf conflict (Investinglive, 01:36 UTC).

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY ~99.10)
factors.summary: USD Index holding gains above 99.00 on Fed hawkish bias and geopolitical de-escalation (FXStreet, 01:59 UTC). Strong dollar helps multinationals (Dow ~50% intl revenue), but headwind for commodity-linked earnings translation.

factors.name: Tech / Chip Sector Weakness
factors.summary: NVDA enters earnings Wed 20 May with moderately bearish event-risk bias (-3.4/+10 score); Korean stocks fell 5% amid chip slump overheating concerns (Bloomberg, 00:37 UTC). Dow has limited direct chip exposure but sentiment spillover matters.

factors.name: Upcoming High-Impact Catalysts
factors.summary: NVDA earnings Wed 20 May; no major Dow component earnings or Fed event disclosed in immediate window. Earnings calendar and data flow remain key unknowns.

factors.name: Oil Price Retracement
factors.summary: WTI fell below $102 on Iran strike postponement (FXStreet, 00:44 UTC), reducing energy inflation pass-through risk to CPI/PCE and input costs for cyclical industrials (CAT, BA, HON, MMM).

factors.name: Price Action: Minimal Momentum
factors.summary: 4h change +0.15%, 24h change -0.01% = flat, no clear directional conviction. Suggests market is still repricing Iran tail risk and yield shock; move is fragile and contested.

factors.name: China Macro / Cross-Border Capital Flows
factors.summary: China resumed foreign inflows in April after March outflow (Bloomberg, 04:44 UTC); PBOC sets USD/CNY at 6.8375 (Investinglive, 01:15 UTC), marginally weaker yuan. Supports China growth narrative but no explosive Dow catalyst visible.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between two competing macro narratives: (1) relief from geopolitical de-escalation (Iran strike postponed) and lower oil prices, which ease input-cost and energy-shock fears for cyclical industrials; and (2) a persistent bond yield shock (10Y flirting with 2007 highs) and hawkish Fed expectations that pressure equity valuations and particularly hurt dividend-sensitive Dow names (financials, utilities, consumer staples). Japan's Q1 GDP beat contrasts with rising inflation-expectations risk noted by RBA, signaling uneven global growth and persistent rate-hike risk. The Dow's +0.15% 4h move and flat 24h change suggest the market is still in repricing mode, with no clear winner between geopolitical relief and yield shock. Upcoming NVDA earnings Wed 20 May and any fresh Fed commentary could shift the balance.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA earnings report Wed 20 May (major tech/AI sentiment barometer, Dow indirect exposure via sentiment)
US economic data (CPI, PCE, durable goods, factory orders) — no specific date given in news, check calendar
FOMC decision / Fed commentary (timeline unclear, monitor for announcement)
Dow component earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, CAT, BA, HD, V — check earnings calendar for this week/next)
China PMI, industrial production data (affects CAT, DE, GE demand outlook)
Oil volatility on Iran headlines (geopolitical risk tail remains live despite postponement)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if (1) fresh Iran military escalation re-ignites oil spike and risk-off selling; (2) Fed signals dovish pivot and yields collapse, reversing the bond rout and supporting equities; (3) Dow component earnings surprise positively and guide forward strength, overcoming yield headwind; or (4) China growth data disappoints sharply, sparking demand recession fears and triggering cyclical selloff.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is contested and flat; avoid chasing. Wait for a retest of 49400–49500 support with positive US/China econ data or Fed dovish signal to consider a long entry, or await a breakdown below 49400 on continued yield shock to confirm a lower-low before shorting. High-impact earnings/data due in <36h cap conviction at 5.

factors.name: Fed Rate Cut Expectations
factors.summary: BNY strategists abandoned their call for two Fed rate cuts in 2026, citing persistent Iran disruption and stronger-than-expected labor market. Fed now seen as 'slowly abandoning easing bias' with potential hike contemplated by mid-2027 per Commerzbank.

factors.name: US Treasury Yields (10y/30y)
factors.summary: 30-year yields at highest since 1999 (per Bloomberg); bond selloff ongoing as inflation fears persist. Treasuries remain 'on edge' as central banks assess response, creating headwinds for dividend-heavy, rate-sensitive Dow names.

factors.name: Inflation Narrative & Oil Supply Risk
factors.summary: Iran war (day 81) creating upward inflation pressure despite Trump's postponement of strikes; Brent crude at ~$109.8 after 2% pullback. OECD warns of 'downward pressure on growth and upward pressure on inflation.' Input costs rising for Dow industrials (CAT, GE, HON).

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD recovering strongly; Barclays argues 'stars aligning for sizable dollar rally this week' on risk-off sentiment and hawkish Fed expectations. DXY near 99.40 breakout. Bullish for multinational Dow revenue translation but defensive tone.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Geopolitical Uncertainty
factors.summary: Markets remain cautious despite Trump's Iran strike delay; BofA survey shows only 4% expecting hard landing, but 66% targeting 6% on 30-year yields. US-Iran talks stalled; Strait of Hormuz closure risk lingering. VIX declining but tail risks remain.

factors.name: China Growth & EM Slowdown
factors.summary: RBA minutes flag inflation and growth risks; softer China data cited by MUFG pressuring AUD. Three Asian 'vulnerable economies' showing rising strains per Bloomberg. Demand headwind for Dow commodity/export-heavy names (CAT, BA, GE).

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings Catalyst
factors.summary: No major Dow earnings in immediate window; NVDA earnings Wed 20-May shows moderately bearish event-risk bias (-3.4/+10). Limited near-term catalyst for individual Dow movers.

factors.name: UK Labor & Global Employment Softening
factors.summary: UK unemployment rose to 5.0% vs 4.9% expected; April payrolls fell -100k (revised). Signals labor market cooling globally, supporting Fed pause narrative but raising recession risk for growth-sensitive Dow names.

factors.name: Price Action (4h/24h)
factors.summary: Dow +0.15% in 4h, -0.01% in 24h — minimal directional conviction. No strong momentum into resistance or support; suggests consolidation after prior volatility.

factors.name: AI Sector Supply Chain Concerns
factors.summary: Iran war exposing weak spots in AI supply chain; chip sector scrambling on material access and rising costs. Potential headwind for tech-linked Dow components (MSFT exposure via index weighting).

screening.narrative: The Dow faces a confluence of headwinds: Fed rate-cut expectations have been scrapped (BNY, Barclays), Treasury yields are at 1999 highs, and inflation pressures from the Iran conflict persist despite Trump's strike delay. While dollar strength is supporting some multinational translation flows, slower China growth, cooling UK labor data, and rising tail risks around energy and supply chains are weighing on cyclical sentiment. The index has barely moved in the past 24h (+0.15% intraday, -0.01% daily), suggesting indecision ahead of major catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: Canada CPI (May 19, ~13:30 UTC) – early inflation signal for North America
NVDA earnings (May 20) – tech sentiment bellwether, risk-off bias flagged
FOMC meeting (late May, exact date unclear from feed) – critical for rate expectations
China economic data releases (if any in next 48h) – demand signal for Dow cyclicals
Iran geopolitical headlines (ongoing Strait of Hormuz risk, negotiations in flux)
US ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (typically late month) – direct Dow composition read
screening.risk_warning: Key invalidation: if Trump-Iran negotiations gain traction and oil rolls over sharply (Brent <$105), Treasury yields compress, and Fed signals potential rate cut patience, Dow could swing bullish quickly. Conversely, if geopolitical flare-up resumes or CPI prints hot again, yields spike further and risk-off accelerates, bias flips decidedly bearish.
screening.entry_guidance: Await retest of 49400–49500 support or a sustained break above 50000 resistance with volume before committing; current consolidation reflects maximum uncertainty. Safer entry on clarity from Canada CPI or earnings whispers.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Trump postponed planned Iran strike after Gulf ally requests for diplomacy (Bloomberg, 10:48). Oil prices fell on delayed strike, reducing supply-shock risk. De-escalation narrative supports risk-on sentiment.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yields climbing above 4.6%, now near 2007 highs (Bloomberg, 11:31). Yields rising pressures Dow's dividend payers and rate-sensitive industrials; higher discount rates reduce forward valuations.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD index rallying on expectations of hawkish Fed policy (gains vs EUR +0.30%, AUD +0.64%, NZD +0.49% per Investinglive 12:08). Stronger dollar supports multinational earnings translation for large-cap Dow names.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings (Home Depot)
factors.summary: Home Depot beat top and bottom-line expectations, raised margin targets, core shopper remains resilient despite higher gas prices (MarketWatch/Bloomberg, 11:02/11:50). Confirms consumer strength and full-year guidance intact.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: WTI near $102.75, up 0.7% in European session but volatility remains high due to Hormuz closure risk and Iran tensions (FXStreet, 09:00). Energy input costs remain elevated; CVX exposure provides mixed hedge.

factors.name: Nasdaq Momentum vs Dow Composition
factors.summary: Nasdaq's bullish momentum stalling (Investinglive, 10:32), semiconductor stocks sliding (Micron, Nvidia), signals broadening weakness beyond mega-cap tech. Dow's industrial/financial tilt less exposed to tech rotation but still vulnerable to macro headwinds.

factors.name: China Growth & Geopolitical Tensions
factors.summary: Weak Chinese data, Putin visiting Beijing (Al Jazeera, 09:13), geopolitical fragmentation. Caterpillar and Boeing exposure to China demand remains under pressure; global trade uncertainty lingers.

factors.name: Equity Futures & Market Sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures down 0.4% as of 7:42 AM (Bloomberg, 11:53); Goldman Sachs notes 'huge opportunity' in volatility, but client flows suggest caution. Early US session pointing lower; profit-taking vs dip-buying balance unclear.

factors.name: AI Execution & Job Market Churn
factors.summary: JPMorgan notes AI moving from hype to real execution (11:00), Anthropic now #1 on CNBC Disruptor list. Standard Chartered cutting 8,000 jobs for AI (10:57). Structural tailwind for tech/productivity, but near-term labor/inflation volatility risk.

factors.name: ECB Hawkish Stance & Global Growth Risk
factors.summary: ECB's Villeroy warns Iran conflict creates dual supply shock risk to growth and inflation (12:06). Global central bankers 'ready to act'; this signals vigilance but also implies downside growth risk for multinational earnings.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between conflicting forces: late-cycle earnings resilience (Home Depot beat, Dow component strength) and macro headwinds (rising yields near 2007 levels, Nasdaq momentum loss, geopolitical overhang). Trump's Iran strike postponement reduced near-term supply shock fears and supported dollar/equities modestly, but 10-year yields climbing above 4.6% and semiconductor weakness signal investor caution. Futures were pointing lower pre-US open, reflecting profit-taking and rotation uncertainty. No major Dow-specific catalyst has broken the stalemate.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC minutes (upcoming this week) – yield expectations and hawkish/dovish signal critical
US flash PMI (Manufacturing & Services) – real-economy health check
Canada CPI (Tuesday per FXStreet 11:00) – spillover to USD/CAD and broader growth sentiment
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI – Dow industrial/financial exposure directly impacted
Upcoming Dow component earnings (JPM, GS, UNH, MSFT, CAT, BA, V if not yet reported)
China PMI/economic data – Caterpillar, Boeing demand signals
US Treasury supply/demand (ECB mentioned record borrower issuance in Europe; bond vigilance rising)
screening.risk_warning: If yields continue climbing above 4.6% without Fed dovish signals, Dow dividend/utility names face de-rating; if Iran tensions re-escalate (new strikes, Hormuz closure confirmed), oil spike would hit consumer demand and industrial margins simultaneously. Conversely, if equity market bottoms ahead of FOMC and yields stabilize, dip-buyers could reignite Dow upside given earnings resilience.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is flat-to-choppy with downside pressure from futures; wait for either a 0.5–1% dip to 49,300–49,450 before considering longs, or hold off entirely until post-FOMC minutes clarity on rate path. Do not chase into weakness given yield/momentum headwinds.

factors.name: Treasury Yields (Long-End)
factors.summary: US 30-year yield hit 5.197%, highest since July 2007 (Bloomberg, FXStreet 15:05). This pressures dividend-heavy Dow holdings (utilities, REITs) and raises discount rates for cyclicals; however, the move may already be pricing in a larger rate-shock narrative.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran Conflict)
factors.summary: Trump postponed planned Iran strike and signaled willingness to negotiate (timeline: 'maybe until early next week'). Oil prices edged lower on deal-relief sentiment; Deutsche Bank noted Iran war is 'missing 3 key ingredients' for a major market selloff, suggesting limited immediate equity contagion risk.

factors.name: Energy & Inflation Inputs
factors.summary: Oil volatility and Strait of Hormuz disruption support energy inflation narrative (negative for real yields, margin pressure on industrials). Conversely, Williams CEO cited strong US nat gas demand from AI/data centers (bullish industrial capex backdrop). Persistent supply-side inflation fears driving long-end yield spike.

factors.name: Technology Sector Weakness
factors.summary: Semiconductor stocks sold off (Nvidia -1.48%, AMD -5.71%, Intel -4.06%) on Tuesday per Investinglive 14:46. MSFT is Dow #2 largest component; tech weakness under higher rates is a headwind for the index's growth/quality anchor.

factors.name: Cyclical Earnings & Forward Guidance
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings or guidance in this news window. Home Depot beat on top/bottom lines (+5% sales) (MarketWatch 15:33), showing resilience; Walmart at all-time high (Bloomberg 15:49). Mixed signals — some defensives holding, but forward visibility on capex/margins clouded by rate backdrop.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: USD broadly firm (DXY eyeing break above 100.00 range per BBH; EURUSD to 1.1593 lows). Dow has ~50% intl revenue; stronger USD headwind for multinational earnings translation, though supports commodity exporters like CVX.

factors.name: Real Estate / Housing
factors.summary: US pending home sales +1.4% MoM vs 1.0% est (Investinglive 14:00), a beat; regional gains mixed (Northeast +6.6%, South -0.7%). Resilience noted, but higher mortgage rates (implied by 30yr yield spike) will cap upside momentum.

factors.name: Fed Policy Uncertainty
factors.summary: Fed's Perli (NYSB) signaled T-bill purchases can be adjusted up or down (Bloomberg 15:20), implying no mechanical easing path. Higher-for-longer rate messaging conflicts with market's earlier hope for pivot; 30yr yield move suggests inflation expectations are rising, not falling.

factors.name: China Trade & Growth Backdrop
factors.summary: No major China PMI or growth releases in window. Trump admin 'not in hurry' to extend China trade truce expiring in November (Bessent comment, Investinglive 14:14); leaves uncertainty for Dow industrials (CAT, BA, etc.) heading into H2 2026.

factors.name: Volatility & Risk Hedging
factors.summary: Bloomberg noted emerging hedging in Russell 2000 (13:24), signaling some small-cap/growth anxiety, but large-cap bullish sentiment intact. VIX not explicitly spiking in headlines; geopolitical risk not yet translated into equity vol.

key_levels.support: 39,200–39,400 (inferred zone; no explicit technical levels in news)
key_levels.resistance: 39,800–40,000 (inferred; recent highs likely in this zone given Walmart ATH and HD beats)

screening.narrative: The Dow faces a mixed, late-cycle backdrop dominated by surging long-end Treasury yields (30yr at 5.197%, highest since 2007) that pressure dividend payers and cyclicals. Geopolitical relief (Iran deal hopes) has eased immediate oil shock risk, but persistent inflation signals and Fed's hawkish stance on rate cuts weigh on growth expectations. Tech weakness (semiconductor selloff) is a headwind for MSFT and other quality anchors, while some defensives (Walmart, Home Depot) show resilience. The combination of higher rates, uncertain forward guidance, and unresolved China trade tensions creates a holding pattern rather than directional conviction.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid fresh entries until rate volatility stabilizes and either earnings confirm resilience or Fed signals relief. If retest support at ~39,200, consider buy-the-dip for longer-term holds; if breaks below, watch for break of 39,000 as next support. Resistance at 40,000 is distant and likely faces selling pressure from current yield backdrop.
catalysts_ahead: Fed speakers (rate expectations continue to drive market)
Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, CAT guidance especially important)
PCE / CPI print (inflation confirmation or surprise could trigger yield repricing)
ISM Manufacturing PMI / Services PMI (cyclical health critical for Dow industrials)
China manufacturing PMI / NBS data (Caterpillar, Boeing demand signal)
Iran negotiations update or new military action threat (oil volatility, risk-off)
Treasury auction results (long-end yield stability/break)
screening.risk_warning: If 30yr yield rolls over sharply (Fed signals imminent easing or deflation scare) OR if Iran deal materializes (safe-haven unwind lifting risk appetite), bias could flip BULLISH quickly. Conversely, if tech earnings disappoint or geopolitical escalates further, Dow could break below 39,000 support. The index's high weight on dividend payers means any further 50bps+ yield rise could trigger a sharper drawdown.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surge (Rate-Sensitive Valuations)
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield topped 5.19%, highest since before 2008 financial crisis; 10-year near 16-month peak. Dow is rate-sensitive (dividend payers, financials), and rising long-end yields compress equity multiples. Energy shock from Iran tensions driving nominal yields sharply higher.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran Escalation)
factors.summary: Trump paused Iran strike but signaled 2-3 days or 'Sunday/early next week' window for negotiation before resumption. On-again-off-again cycle creates supply-chain uncertainty and energy cost inflation. Oil at $103.20 (+1.16% on day), up for 4th consecutive day—input cost pressure on industrials.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Energy Inflation
factors.summary: WTI extends 4-day rally amid Strait of Hormuz supply concerns; mortgage rates hit highest since July on energy-driven inflation fears. Trump administration considering gas tax holiday, signaling concern over consumer impact. Elevated energy costs weigh on consumer discretionary and transport costs for Dow components.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (Multinational Headwind)
factors.summary: USD Index at >1-month highs on Iran tensions and hawkish Fed bets; EUR/USD fell to 1.1593 (lowest since April 8). Dow has ~50% international revenue exposure—stronger dollar headwind for multinational earnings translation (CAT, BA, MSFT, etc.).

factors.name: Fed Policy Shift (Warsh Messaging)
factors.summary: Trump signals he'll let incoming Fed chair Kevin Warsh 'do what he wants to do' on rates—easing prior pressure for cuts. However, bond market vigilantes now testing Warsh resolve; combined with inflation shock, Fed likely held to pause or hawkish hold. Reduces near-term cut expectations, supportive of higher yields.

factors.name: Home Depot Beat (Consumer Resilience Check)
factors.summary: HD beat expectations and maintained full-year guidance; core shopper described as 'resilient' despite higher gas prices. Sales rose 5%. Suggests consumer not yet breaking, though larger project pullback noted. Mixed signal—baseline holding but forward visibility questioned.

factors.name: Bond Vigilante Pressure & Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Global bond markets on edge; mortgage rates highest since July per MarketWatch. Financial conditions tightening, real yields rising on energy shock. Hawkish Fed expectations embedded (XAG/USD under pressure on stronger USD and rising yields). Equities vulnerable to multiple compression.

factors.name: AI Momentum & Tech Strength (Offsetting Factor)
factors.summary: Google announced advanced AI models and agentic tools; Anthropic hired OpenAI co-founder Karpathy; SpaceX IPO heating up Wall Street interest. MSFT and tech-heavy Dow components (AAPL indirect via index weight) seeing AI tailwinds. Partially offsets macro headwinds.

factors.name: ISM/PMI Data, Economic Calendar Upcoming
factors.summary: FOMC minutes and crude oil inventories due Wednesday; no major ISM or manufacturing PMI releases in immediate 24h window. Durable goods, factory orders data not yet released in this window. Forward calendar event risk remains, limiting conviction.

factors.name: Price Action (4h / 24h Change)
factors.summary: Dow down -0.16% in 4h, -0.22% in 24h; S&P 500 -0.22%, Nasdaq -0.40%. Index briefly challenged 50K last week but retreated. Technicals show mild downside momentum; move not yet extended but directional bias negative on macro catalyst mix.

screening.narrative: The Dow is experiencing a PHASE 2 selloff driven by a confluence of macro headwinds: surging long-end Treasury yields (30-year at 5.19%, highest pre-GFC), geopolitical Iran escalation risk creating energy cost inflation (WTI +1.16%, mortgage rates soaring), and a stronger USD (headwind for Dow's 50% multinational exposure). While Home Depot's resilience and AI momentum offer some support, the bond vigilante response to inflation expectations and Fed policy uncertainty is compressing equity multiples. The index is down modestly but directionally aligned with rate/inflation shock.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Minutes (Wednesday, May 20) — key for Fed intent and inflation guidance
Crude oil inventory data (Wednesday) — supply-chain signal from Iran tensions
ISM Manufacturing PMI (next week) — Dow industrial composition heavily exposed
Iran negotiation developments (daily/hourly) — Trump window 'by Sunday/early next week'
Treasury auction results and 10-year yield trajectory (ongoing) — rate-sensitive equity valuation driver
Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, CAT guidance) — forward visibility into industrial/financial health
screening.risk_warning: If Iran negotiations produce a breakthrough deal (easing supply fears), oil falls sharply, and Treasury yields roll over, this SELL bias inverts to BULLISH. Additionally, if upcoming ISM PMI or earnings beat expectations and Fed signals dovish tilt, the Dow could retest 50K quickly. Warsh confirmation of 'patient' stance (vs. hawkish hold) would be a relief rally trigger. Watch for any pullback in USD or long yields as invalidation of the bearish setup.
screening.entry_guidance: Wait for pullback toward 49100–49200 (support zone) before considering short entry; move is already underway (-0.22% in 24h, PHASE 2), so chasing current weakness is risky. Monitor FOMC minutes and Iran headlines closely for volatility. If yields stabilize and USD momentum stalls, tactical long at 49300 with tight stop below 49000 on mean-reversion is viable. Avoid hard shorts into FOMC (event risk can whipsaw).

factors.name: Treasury yields surge
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield topped 5.19% (highest since pre-2008 financial crisis) and 10-year near 16-month peak. Rising long-end yields pressure dividend-heavy Dow components and rate-sensitive financials.

factors.name: Oil shock and inflation concerns
factors.summary: Crude inventory plunge signals demand, but geopolitical Iran war uncertainty is driving energy costs higher and resurgent inflation fears. Input cost pressures weigh on industrials (CAT, BA, HON exposure).

factors.name: Broad equity weakness
factors.summary: S&P 500 down -0.67%, NASDAQ down -0.84%, Russell 2000 down -1.01% on session. Tech and small-cap led declines; Dow lagging less badly (-0.65%) suggests relative defensive rotation, not strength.

factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (Iran)
factors.summary: Trump postponed strikes but threatened 'another hit' within days if ceasefire talks fail. WSJ reports 'little progress' in negotiations. Uncertainty in energy/supply chains and potential military spending drag investor risk appetite.

factors.name: USD strength
factors.summary: Dollar rallying on strong ADP jobs data (42,250 avg. private jobs, strongest since Oct 2025) and higher yields. However, USD strength is a mixed signal for multinational Dow exporters (negative translation headwind).

factors.name: Fed rate expectations shifting hawkish
factors.summary: Traders pricing in rate *hike* odds by July 2027 (per prediction markets) amid oil-driven inflation concerns. Bond market 'capitulation' selloff signals rising real yields, pressure on valuation multiples.

factors.name: Earnings mixed, guidance stable
factors.summary: Home Depot kept FY outlook intact (positive signal), Toll Brothers beat earnings. But forward guidance from cyclical leaders (CAT, DE, HON, GE) not yet front-and-center in news flow; limited clarity on demand.

factors.name: China macro softness
factors.summary: April China data shows soft retail sales and sharp fall in Fixed Asset Investment per HSBC. Weak China growth headwind for Boeing, Caterpillar, materials demand; limits upside for Dow cyclicals.

factors.name: VIX / volatility backdrop
factors.summary: No explicit VIX spike reported, but geopolitical uncertainty (Iran, Ukraine, Ebola concerns) and bond market turmoil suggest latent risk-off conditions. Credit spreads not explicitly widening yet, but surveillance warranted.

factors.name: Prior bias history consistency
factors.summary: Today's 19:00 read was SELL 6/10 (no confirmation). Fresh SELL at 5/10 reflects *softening* conviction — move is in early phase, support for conviction is present but not overwhelming.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught in a broader equity selloff (-0.65% on session) driven by a perfect storm: surging long-end Treasury yields (30Y at 5.19%, 10Y at 16-month highs), oil-shock inflation fears (Iran war escalation risk, crude inventory plunge), and hawkish Fed repricing (traders now pricing rate *hike* odds by mid-2027). While USD strength and solid US jobs data (ADP 42k avg.) provide some support, multinational Dow heavyweights face translation headwinds and cyclical demand concerns from weak China data. Home Depot's steady guidance is a rare bright spot, but it is insufficient to offset geopolitical and inflation-driven risk-off. The sell-off is in Phase 2 — move is modest and underway, but conviction remains mixed.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Warsh confirmation/remarks (imminent — takes helm later this week)
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (if released next week — key Dow industrial signal)
Durable goods orders / factory orders (next week — CAT, BA, industrials barometer)
China economic data (ongoing — April softness, May releases ahead)
Iran ceasefire negotiations (Trump said 2–3 days or 'Sunday/early next week' — high volatility risk)
Oil price action (WTI, Brent — energy input costs, inflation signal)
Treasury auction results & 10Y yield stability (ongoing bond market stress)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran ceasefire agreement is reached (defusing geopolitical premium), Treasury yields stabilize below 5.10%, or a major Dow component (e.g., UNH, MSFT, JPM) posts upside guidance, bias flips NEUTRAL/BUY. Additionally, if the bond selloff reverses (flight-to-quality rally), real yields could fall sharply, lifting equities. VIX spike or credit spread widening would *confirm* SELL; their absence weakens the case.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is early (PHASE 2, only -0.65% session). Do not chase shorts; wait for retest of 49,400–49,500 resistance or break below 49,150 support to confirm direction. Better entry on pullback to 49,300–49,350 if bias remains SELL.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surge (Rate-Sensitive Pain)
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield hit 5.19%, highest since pre-2008 financial crisis (per Bloomberg 21:00 UTC). Long-end yields crush dividend-heavy Dow components (utilities, REITs, high-yielding industrials like JNJ, PG). This is THE dominant headwind for a yield-sensitive, mature index.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations Rising (Oil-Driven)
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'mounting inflation concerns' pushing yields higher. ECB's Kocher warns June hike 'unavoidable' if Hormuz Strait stays closed. Mortgage rates hit highest since July (per MarketWatch 20:06). Input cost pressures threaten Dow industrials' margins.

factors.name: Broad Market Selloff (Risk-Off Spillover)
factors.summary: S&P 500 down -0.67%, NASDAQ -0.84%, Russell 2000 -1.01% (per investingLive 20:21). Tech and small-caps led decline on rising yields. Dow lagged but still negative; this mirrors phase-2 broad weakness.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran/Middle East Unresolved)
factors.summary: WSJ reports 'little progress' in US-Iran talks (18:37 UTC). Trump remains ambiguous ('may have to give Iran another hit'). Hormuz Strait closure risk keeps energy volatility high, threatens supply chains and inflation expectations for Q2-Q3.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (Mixed for Dow Multinationals)
factors.summary: USD Index rallying toward 99.30 on strong ADP jobs data and higher yields (per FXStreet 20:22). Dollar strength supports earnings translation for Dow's ~50% international revenue but offsets gains from lower equity risk premiums.

factors.name: Dow-Specific Earnings (Weak Signals)
factors.summary: Home Depot held guidance but customer weakness visible in discretionary segments (per MarketWatch 20:35). Limited positive surprises. Fiscal headwinds (higher mortgage rates, energy costs) visible.

factors.name: Fed Rate Path Unclear (Warsh Transition Risk)
factors.summary: Trump shifting tone on Fed—'let Warsh do what he wants' (per MarketWatch 20:18). Markets now pricing rising odds of rate hike by July 2027 (per FXStreet 20:10). Kevin Warsh takes helm facing conflicting pressure: White House wants cuts, bond market is screaming higher yields.

factors.name: Phase of Move (4h: -0.41%, 24h: -0.37%)
factors.summary: Decline is modest and gradual, not yet extended. No capitulation or reversal signs. Typical Phase 2 consolidation into afternoon close. Not late-stage, but momentum is downside.

factors.name: Bias History (SELL3/10, SELL6/10, SELL5/10)
factors.summary: Prior three reads consistently leaned SELL despite low-to-moderate confidence (5-6/10). No confirmations triggered. This suggests analyst uncertainty despite directional lean. Today's data (yields, geopolitics) provides more concrete bearish evidence.

factors.name: Cass Freight, Durable Goods (Absent/Dated)
factors.summary: No recent hard data on freight, orders, or industrial production in news feed. GM shifts Mexico assembly (slight reshoring positive, but not enough to offset macro headwinds). Data gap limits conviction.

screening.narrative: The Dow's selloff is driven primarily by surging long-end Treasury yields (30Y at 5.19%, highest since 2007) triggered by renewed inflation fears and Middle East geopolitical risk (Iran talks stalled). This is deeply negative for a dividend-heavy, rate-sensitive index where many components (financials, utilities) derive yield appeal from low rates. While the decline itself (-0.41% in 4h, -0.37% in 24h) is modest and not yet extended, the structural headwinds—higher mortgage rates, energy input cost fears, and unresolved Hormuz closure risk—suggest further weakness ahead. Dow heavyweights like JNJ, PG, JPM, and GS are particularly vulnerable to sustained yield acceleration. Fed policy remains a wild card: Warsh transition is creating uncertainty, and markets now price in rate hike odds, which is the opposite of what equity bulls need.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh confirmation/commentary (imminent, likely this week)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (May, likely next week) — direct read on Dow industrial demand
ISM Services PMI (next week) — cyclical turn signal
Durable goods orders / factory orders (monthly data) — Caterpillar, Boeing sensitivity
CPI/PCE release (late May) — critical for yield expectations
ECB June 11 meeting — if Hormuz stays closed, rate hike likely, triggering USD/yield spike
Dow component earnings updates (JPM, GS, UNH, etc.) — guidance on cost inflation
Oil price stabilization or escalation (Iran war headlines ongoing)
screening.risk_warning: If Treasury yields reverse sharply downward (flight to safety, Fed pivot signal, or Iran truce breakthrough), the Dow could snap back hard—dividend stocks would rally. If oil prices collapse on demand fears or geopolitical resolution, inflation concerns ease and yields fall. Also, a surprise hawkish Fed pivot or hawkish Warsh comment could support higher yields *and* equities briefly due to stagflation premium. Watch for capitulation lows or technical support holds (49,000) that could trigger mean-reversion bounces.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway but not extended; if selling continues, wait for retest of 49,000–49,100 support before considering shorts. If yields stabilize and 49,600 resistance is tested again without break, look for SELL signal into retest.
